message:
feat(vdbe): pipeline stage profiling markers + pipeline_stages bench

Introduces lightweight, trace-gated VDBE pipeline stage profiling
so we can measure decode / execute / commit stage latencies
independently and track them over time via a criterion bench.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/lib.rs

- New private enum VdbePipelineStage { Decode, Execute, Commit }
  with an as_str() constant method.  Used in the structured trace
  event payloads as the canonical stage label.
- New VdbeProfileMarker RAII struct: records Instant::now() on
  entry and on Drop emits a single
  `tracing::trace!(target: "fsqlite_vdbe::profile", stage=...,
  event="end", elapsed_ns=...)` event.  Ignored entirely when the
  `fsqlite_vdbe::profile` TRACE level isn't enabled — the marker's
  Option<Instant> is left None and Drop is a no-op.  This keeps
  the overhead of compiled-in profiling to a single tracing level
  check per stage entry in release builds.
- enter_vdbe_profile_stage(stage) is marked #[inline(never)] so
  tracing filters can suppress the whole function in the common
  "profile disabled" path without the compiler duplicating the
  check across three call sites.
- Three wrapper helpers expose the marker to the rest of the crate:
    enter_vdbe_decode_profile_stage()
    enter_vdbe_execute_profile_stage()
    enter_vdbe_commit_profile_stage()
  Both the module-private _(crate)_ variants for instrumenting the
  engine and a set of `pub` profile_vdbe_{decode,execute,commit}_stage(f)
  closure wrappers for use from benches / external callers who
  want to measure a block of code as one stage.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/Cargo.toml
- Register a new harness-less criterion bench:
    [[bench]]
    name = "pipeline_stages"
    harness = false

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/benches/pipeline_stages.rs (new)

- A criterion bench that measures each pipeline stage under a few
  representative parameter sweeps:
    EXECUTE_STAGE_OP_REPEATS = [64, 256, 1024]
      — number of opcodes executed inside a single
        profile_vdbe_execute_stage block, so the overhead of the
        stage marker itself is amortized the way the real engine
        amortizes it.
    COMMIT_STAGE_DIRTY_PAGES = [2, 8, 32]
      — number of dirty pages flushed inside a single
        profile_vdbe_commit_stage block.
- The decode-stage case uses serialize_record() to build a
  SqliteValue record, then measures parse_record() inside
  profile_vdbe_decode_stage at a few column counts so the "record
  decode" hot path has its own independent regression signal.
- Uses MemoryVfs + SimplePager + SimpleTransaction so the bench
  doesn't touch the real disk path; we want to isolate the stage
  profiling overhead from VFS latency.
- Wrapped with Criterion's BatchSize::SmallInput + Throughput
  measurements so criterion's output reports ops/s and
  pages-committed/s without the harness boilerplate having to
  compute them by hand.

No engine-side instrumentation lands in this commit — the engine
call sites that actually enter the new profile markers come with
the "vdbe engine collation-aware UNIQUE constraints" follow-up in
this same batch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -63,6 +63,10 @@ harness = false
 name = "make_record"
 harness = false
 
+[[bench]]
+name = "pipeline_stages"
+harness = false
+
 # Cannot inherit workspace lints because workspace forbids unsafe_code
 # and the SIMD MakeRecord helper requires a tightly scoped unsafe island.
 [lints.rust]
